---
title: "More Kew specimens from Nina's product page"
author:
  name: Lemuria
  affiliation:
    - name: Independent researcher; Manila, Philippines
  email: lemuria@lemuria.ph
license: CC BY-SA
journal:
  title: "Lemuria's (Informal) Journal of Clothing Taxonomy"
date: 2026-03-26
lang: en
execute: 
  freeze: true
# publisher-id: "lict2026.0006"
abstract: |
  We build on the monograph of the Kew dresses with additional descriptions for the Kew dress specimens on Nina Lee's product pages; including the first skirt species description in the entire taxonomy, *Kewisicasa laria*. In total, we describe new 14 species and new subgenera.
bibliography: ninas_kews.bib
number-sections: true
citation:
  author: Lemuria
  # citation-key: lemuria_kew_2026
  title: "More Kew specimens from Nina's product page"
  original-date: 2026-03-26
  publisher: Lemuria
  # article-id: lict2026.0006
  publisher-place: Philippines
format:
  html:
    format-links: false
    # other-links:
    #   - text: PDF
    #     href: https://static.lemuria.ph/lct01/pdf/2026/2026.0006_gunnelaurentia_johnsonia.pdf
    #     icon: file-pdf
  aft-pdf:
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{forest}
keywords: kew dress, kewoidea, kewthidae, helikewidae, nina lee, clothing taxonomy, home sewing, sewing pattern
---

```{=latex}
\begin{multicols}{2}
```

## Background
The Kew dress is now perhaps the most extensively-studied sewing pattern in the LCT-01. Earlier in March 2026, we published a 52-page monograph on the Kew dresses, which laid out the structure of Kew dress taxonomy and described over 47 species [@lemuria_taxonomy_2026]. However, the large amount of Kew dresses on the Internet threatened to inflate the scope of @lemuria_taxonomy_2026 excessively; as such, we enacted a "species freeze" where new Kew specimens would be reserved for subsequent papers. We continue our research into the Kew dress as part of this paper.

When authoring the Kew monograph, we focused primarily on makes of the pattern in the wild as opposed to official product photos, for a variety of reasons, such as not wanting to have the appearance of a reseller catalog^[This concern is a holdover from our internal MediaWiki site, where we embed images more freely than in the *Journal*. The academic paper format of the *Journal* extensively distinguishes it from a reseller listing or catalog.], and the richer documentation third parties produced compared to official product pages.

Size is typically not considered a relevant morphological character for taxa due to its continuous, non-discrete nature. However, the sale of size ranges of sewing patterns as separate products constitutes useful and discrete phylogenetic information, which is best suited for defining infrageneric taxa. The usage of infrageneric ranks originated in *Heleonora* Lemuria, 2026 (unpublished so far), encompassing the sleeveless variant of the Eleonora by Silversaga Patterns.

```{=latex}
\end{multicols}
```
## Trees

As with the hybrid phenetic&ndash;phylogenetic structure of the LCT-01, the Kew 

::: {#fig-kewthidae}
```{r}
library(ggtree)

tree <- read.tree(text = "(((K. hawkinsia,K. caledonia,K. eleanorae,K. lauraflora)K. subg. Expansa)Kewthea)Kewthidae;")

ggtree(tree, layout = "rectangular") +
  geom_tiplab(size = 3, fontface = "italic") +
  geom_text(data = td_filter(!isTip), aes(label = label),
            size = 2, hjust = 1.1, vjust = -0.4) +
  hexpand(0.5) + vexpand(0.8)
```

A tree of *Kewthea* subg. *Expansa*
:::

## Descriptions
### *Helikewisia*
***Helikewisia*** Lemuria, 2026

#### *Helikewisia roseatulipa*
***Helikewisia roseatulipa*** Lemuria, 2026; sp. nov.

### *Kewisicasa*
***Kewisicasa*** Lemuria, 2026

#### *Kewisicasa laria*
***Kewisicasa laria*** Lemuria, 2026; sp. nov.

**Holotype**: *Kew 14201.147KL* <!-- No name -->

**Etymology**: Pseudo-Latin feminine form of larus ("gull"), from the genus *Larus* Linnaeus, 1758.

#### *Kewisicasa jessae* {#sec-kewisicasa-jessae}
***Kewisicasa jessae*** Lemuria, 2026; sp. nov.

**Holotype**: *Jess 29081.31.58KJ*

**Etymology**:

**Diagnosis**:

#### *Kewisicasa mandia* {#sec-kewisicasa-mandia}
***Kewisicasa mandia*** Lemuria, 2026; sp. nov.

**Holotype**: *Mandy 210535.158.3903KM*

**Etymology**:

**Diagnosis**:

### *Kewisia* subg. *Ninae* {#sec-kewisia-subg-ninae}
***Kewisia* subg. *Ninae*** Lemuria, 2026; subgen. nov.

#### *Kewisia tropiki* {#sec-kewisia-tropiki}
***Kewisia tropiki*** Lemuria, 2026; sp. nov.

**Holotype**: *Nina 2017.013.8377KT* &mdash; Photograph.

* **Page**: See @kew6_20
* **SHA2-256**: `42929bfbdf49b423201a3650623216e0cb08cbc41299a1c6f8b555f68ff5fea7`
* **Raw image URLs**:
  * **Original**: <https://www.ninalee.co.uk/cdn/shop/products/Kew-V2-Nina-1.jpg?v=1680151814>
  * **IA**: <https://web.archive.org/web/20230714101532/https://www.ninalee.co.uk/cdn/shop/products/Kew-V2-Nina-1.jpg?v=1680151814>

**Diagnosis**: *K. tropiki* is characterized by a ventral medial button row of 15 circular two thread-hole buttons of a primarily ivory color with slight dark blue accents at random angles, and a consistent vertical threadline; and a fabric with a pink background with blue leaves, and red and purple flowers, the "Tropical Stems" fabric from Lady McElroy.

**Etymology**: 

#### *Kewisia roseafolia* {#sec-kewisia-roseafolia}
***Kewisia roseafolia*** Lemuria, 2026; sp. nov.

**Holotype**: *Nina 2017.013.8289KR* &mdash; Photograph.

* **Page**: See @kew6_20
* **SHA2-256**: `f4c34767bc2a237da8cfd304e052e3dfa7b97ee57dc0feba8116c11e00405f0a`
* **Raw image URLs**:
  * **Original**: <https://www.ninalee.co.uk/cdn/shop/products/Kew-Dress-V2-9.jpg?v=1680151814>
  * **IA**: <https://web.archive.org/web/20260326192109/https://www.ninalee.co.uk/cdn/shop/products/Kew-Dress-V2-9.jpg?v=1680151814>

**Diagnosis**: *K. roseafolia* is characterized by a ventral medial button row of 15 circular two thread-hole buttons of a wooden color, and a fabric with stylized depictions of leaves in pink and white.

**Etymology**: From Latin *rosea*, feminine form of *roseus* "pink, rose-colored" + *folia*, pseudo-Latin feminine form of *folium* "leaf".

### *Kewthea* subg. *Nina*
#### *Kewthea ruberabotonia* {#sec-kewthea-ruberabotonia}
***Kewthea ruberabotonia*** Lemuria, 2026; sp. nov.

**Holotype**: *Kew 4109.187KR* &mdash; Photograph.
3543&times;5315.
EXIF metadata stripped.
*Kewthea ruberabotonia* wb. Amy, c. 2017.
&copy; Nina Chang-Smith or unknown photographer, 2017, all rights reserved.

* **Page**: See @kew6_20
* **SHA2-256**: `4b13414afd28f3149ae852081a83dddc00f48b53e2263023726a7405762143ef`
* **Raw image URLs**:
  * **Original**: <https://www.ninalee.co.uk/cdn/shop/products/Kew-V1-Amy-close-up-1.jpg?v=1680151814>
  * **IA**: <https://web.archive.org/web/20230714160745/https://www.ninalee.co.uk/cdn/shop/products/Kew-V1-Amy-close-up-1.jpg?v=1680151814>

**Etymology**: From pseudo-Latin *rubera*, corrupted form of *rubra*, feminine form of *ruber* ("red"). Also interpretable as *ruber* ("red") + *-a-* (epenthetic vowel added for euphony) + *botonia* ("button").

**Remarks**:

#### *Kewthea venetaflora* {#sec-kewthea-venetaflora}
***Kewthea venetaflora*** Lemuria, 2026; sp. nov.

**Holotype**: *Kew 4109.124KV* &mdash; Photograph.
1937&times;2657.
EXIF metadata stripped.
*Kewthea venetaflora* wb. Amy (`W/2026 F7`), c. 2017.
&copy; Nina Chang-Smith or unknown photographer, 2017, all rights reserved.

* **Page**: See @kew6_20
* **SHA2-256**: `6d12a063eead31810dde4f7dbac040cf5e7fd1ba25d1fd5f601d21c2d4e3b378`
* **Raw image URLs**:
  * **Original**: <https://www.ninalee.co.uk/cdn/shop/products/Kew-Dress-V1-4.jpg?v=1680151814>
  * **IA**: <https://web.archive.org/web/20260326181258/https://www.ninalee.co.uk/cdn/shop/products/Kew-Dress-V1-4.jpg?v=1680151814>

**Etymology**: From Latin *veneta*, feminine form of *venetus* ("sky blue") + pseudo-Latin *flora* "flower", from Latin *Flora*, the goddess of flowers.

### *Kewthea* subg. *Expansa* {#sec-kewthea-subg-expansa}
***Kewthea* subg. *Expansa*** Lemuria, 2026; subgen. nov.

**Diagnosis**: Phylogenetically, *Kewthea* subg. *Expansa* is defined by origin from size 21 and above of the tea dress (Kewthidae) variant of the Kew pattern.

#### *Kewthea hawkinsia* {#sec-kewthea-hawkinsia}
***Kewthea hawkinsia*** Lemuria, 2026; sp. nov.

**Holotype**: *Laura 2023.001.1682KH* &mdash;
Photograph. 1871&times;2551.
EXIF metadata stripped.
*Kewthea hawkinsia* wb. Laura.Haw., 2023.
&copy; Laura Hawkins or unknown photographer, 2023, all rights reserved.

* **Page**: See @kew16_28
* **SHA2-256**: `e56fa1e776eb9a6cba4e98059778e9df903ab31613e39ad7c72422d4374dfe39`
* **Raw image URLs**
  * **Original**: <https://www.ninalee.co.uk/cdn/shop/products/Laura-Hawkins-Kew-Dress.jpg?v=1677871695>
  * **IA**: <https://web.archive.org/web/20260326050209/https://www.ninalee.co.uk/cdn/shop/products/Laura-Hawkins-Kew-Dress.jpg?v=1677871695>

**Diagnosis**: *K. hawkinsia* is characterized by a ventral medial button row of 15 heart-shaped two thread-hole buttons, and a fabric with a light white&ndash;pink background and a stylized red flower with two petals and black dots scattered in a roughly rectangular shape with curved edges.

**Etymology**: From *Hawkins* + *-ia*, feminine abstract noun suffix.

#### *Kewthea caledonia* {#sec-kewthea-caledonia}
***Kewthea caledonia*** Lemuria, 2026; sp. nov.

**Holotype**: *Emma 2023.001.1402KC* &mdash;
Photograph. 1440&times;2560.
EXIF metadata stripped. 
*Kewthea caledonia* wb. Emma (`W/2026 F5`), c. 2023.
&copy; Emma or unknown photographer, c. 2023, all rights reserved.

* **Page**: See @kew16_28
* **SHA2-256**: `4e650f4804476216e169e757f5eecf9a9fb87279ebda2e477b8bbc0b5e59644f`
* **Raw image URLs**:
  * **Original**: <https://www.ninalee.co.uk/cdn/shop/products/Sew-Do-It-Emma-Nina-Lee-Kew-Dress-16-28-scaled.jpg?v=1677871695>
  * **IA**: <https://web.archive.org/web/20260326051449/https://www.ninalee.co.uk/cdn/shop/products/Sew-Do-It-Emma-Nina-Lee-Kew-Dress-16-28-scaled.jpg?v=1677871695>

**Diagnosis**: *K. caledonia* is characterized by a ventral medial button row of ~15 small buttons.^[Image resolution insufficient to identify buttons.], and a fabric with a dark-blue background and numerous variably-sized sky blue, purple, and pale bright orange dots scattered at random.

**Etymology**: From Latin *Caledonia* ("Scotland"), from Emma's location.

#### *Kewthea eleanorae* {#sec-kewthea-eleanorae}
***Kewthea eleanorae*** Lemuria, 2026; sp. nov.

**Holotype**: *Eleanor 2023.001.8404KE* &mdash;
Photograph. 1440&times;2560.
EXIF metadata stripped.
*Kewthea eleanorae* wb. Eleanor (`W/2026 F3`), c. 2023.
&copy; Eleanor or unknown photographer, c. 2023, all rights reserved.

* **Page**: See @kew16_28
* **SHA2-256**: `e15a799d0f7b20bc03da95bfcf0cf0f8fed33443fa70cda44bbf35bba2265bdd`
* **Raw image URLs**:
  * **Original**: <https://www.ninalee.co.uk/cdn/shop/products/Eleanor-Nina-Lee-Kew-Dress-16-28-scaled.jpg?v=1677871695>
  * **IA**: <https://web.archive.org/web/20260326051053/https://www.ninalee.co.uk/cdn/shop/products/Eleanor-Nina-Lee-Kew-Dress-16-28-scaled.jpg?v=1677871695>

**Diagnosis**: *K. eleanorae* is characterized by a ventral medial button row of 10 circular buttons with no thread holes, four on the bodice and six on the skirt; and a plain dark blue fabric.

**Etymology**: From *Eleanor* + *-ae*, feminine genitive suffix.

#### *Kewthea lauraflora* {#sec-kewthea-lauraflora}
***Kewthea lauraflora*** Lemuria, 2026; sp. nov.

**Holotype**: *Laura 2023.001.4901KL* &mdash;
Photograph. 1920&times;2560.
EXIF metadata stripped.
*Kewthea lauraflora* wb. Laura (`W/2026 F4`), c. 2023.
&copy; Laura or unknown photographer, c. 2023, all rights reserved.

* **Page**: See @kew16_28
* **SHA2-256**: `e03d4374644fb22f63d97ccc4d5ca523f35889c85666bda316040e7174ec3404`
* **Raw image URLs**:
  * **Original**: <https://www.ninalee.co.uk/cdn/shop/products/Laura-Nina-Lee-Kew-Dress-16-28-scaled.jpg>
  * **IA**: <https://web.archive.org/web/20260326192448/https://www.ninalee.co.uk/cdn/shop/products/Laura-Nina-Lee-Kew-Dress-16-28-scaled.jpg>

**Diagnosis**: *K. lauraflora* is characterized by

**Etymology**: From *Laura*, pseudo-Latin *flora*, to distinguish from *Kewthea hawkinsia* wb. Laura.Haw., from Latin *Flora*, the goddess of flowers.

```{=latex}
\begin{multicols}{2}
```

## Appendix

### Person abbreviations
The following abbreviations have been defined in @lemuria_taxonomy_2026:
**Lemuria**,
**Nina**,

In this paper, we establish the new person abbreviations:

* **Laura.Haw.** &mdash; Laura Hawkins, pattern tester for the Kew expansion pack

We establish the new alphanumeric designations:

* **Eleanor** (`W/2026 F3`), wearer of *Kewthea eleanorae*
* **Laura** (`W/2026 F4`), wearer of *Kewthea lauraflora*
* **Emma** (`W/2026 F5`), wearer of *Kewthea caledonia*
* **Amy** (`W/2026 F7`), wearer of *Kewthea ruberabotonia*

### Image specimens

#### New image specimens

### Information
*Lemuria's (Informal) Journal of Clothing Taxonomy* is the gray literature journal of the First Lemurian Clothing Taxonomy, a system by Lemuria to assign Latin names to dresses using biological methods. The author, known mononymically as just Lemuria, is an Filipino programmer and independent gray literature researcher from Manila.

**Disclaimer**: *Lemuria's (Informal) Journal of Clothing Taxonomy* is a non-peer-reviewed, single-author, citizen science/gray literature publication with no institutional backing.

<!-- Publisher article ID: 2026.0007 -->

At publication, the journal did not have a DOI prefix.

Work began on the paper on 2026-03-26 and was published the same day.

Visit us at: <https://lct01.lemuria.ph>

&copy; Lemuria 2026. All original text and image content by Lemuria is licensed under CC BY-SA 4.0, unless explicitly noted. Linked images and other content are &copy; their respective owners or copyright holders.

### References

::: {#refs}
:::

```{=latex}
\end{multicols}
```
